title: Overlay pin icon and X button on pinned video thumbnail

requirements.text: In the PinnedVideo component, overlay the unpin (X) button absolutely at the top-right corner of the video container (top-2 right-2), styled as a small circular button (bg-black/50 text-white rounded-full w-6 h-6) with a hover effect (hover:bg-black/70). Remove any separate header row or dedicated section that previously housed the unpin button.

quotes: overlay the x to remove the pin on the top right of the video

acceptanceCriteria: The X (unpin) button is absolutely positioned at the top-right corner of the pinned video container.
The button is styled as a small circular icon button with a semi-transparent dark background.
Hovering the button darkens its background.
Clicking the button unpins the video (calls useUnpinVideo mutation).
No separate header row for the unpin button exists; the space previously used by it is reclaimed.

requirements.text: In the PinnedVideo component, add a non-clickable pin icon (Lucide Pin icon) absolutely overlaid at the top-left corner of the video container (top-2 left-2), styled identically to the X button (bg-black/50 text-white rounded-full w-6 h-6) as a visual indicator that the video is pinned.

quotes: pin icon could sit at top-2 left-2 (opposite corner from the X)
X button + pin icon overlay (both corners)

acceptanceCriteria: A Lucide Pin icon is absolutely positioned at the top-left corner of the pinned video container.
The pin icon is styled with the same circular semi-transparent dark background as the X button.
The pin icon is non-interactive (no click handler, pointer-events-none).
Both icons (pin top-left, X top-right) are visible simultaneously over the video embed.

constraints: Do not modify any immutable frontend paths (hooks, main.tsx, ui components).
Only the PinnedVideo component should be changed; do not alter ChatArea or other components unless strictly necessary to remove the old unpin button header row.
nonGoals: Adding any other controls or metadata overlays to the pinned video.
Changes to the video embed itself or its sizing.
